title: Pop song and ACs audio commentary
display_title: Pop song and ACs audio commentary
wikitext:
[]: Pop song and AC's audio commentary             Quote Post by Ark » Tue Jul 29, 2008 2:26 pm Hi there. This is my first time posting on Evamonkey/geeks. Though I did post a lot about Eva on the AN forum a couple of years ago. Anyway I'm currently rewatching Eva and have a couple of questions. 1) Does the j-pop song you always hear being played in the show have a name? 2) Does anyone have those audio commentaries for eps 1-6 that Aaron Clark had posted on Evamonkey? It was those that made me want to revisit the series after watching the film years ago. Many thanks

[]:             Quote Post by Formless One » Tue Jul 29, 2008 3:35 pm 1) which one, specifically? I know the opening theme is called Cruel Angels Thesis, and it was made specifically for the show. 2) Ask someone else.

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Tue Jul 29, 2008 4:53 pm Ark wrote: Does anyone have those audio commentaries for eps 1-6 that Aaron Clark had posted on Evamonkey? It was those that made me want to revisit the series after watching the film years ago.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Tue Jul 29, 2008 10:31 pm Sup Ark. I don't see them linked from the front page anymore, but you could probably send him a PM or dig through archive.org.

[]:             Quote Post by Fin.de.Siecle » Wed Jul 30, 2008 2:39 am I know I have the fifth one, and I'm pretty sure I have some of the others on a USB drive somewhere...

[]:             Quote Post by Ark » Wed Jul 30, 2008 2:52 pm Thanks for all the responses. I've found the commentaries now. By the way the song I'm talking about is the one that Shinji is often playing on his SDAT player. It's also playing in public a lot (e.g. In the store that Asuka runs to after failing in musical twister).

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Wed Jul 30, 2008 3:04 pm Hmm... Background Music II?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Jul 30, 2008 4:25 pm 1) If you mean on Shinji's SDAT after episode 2 ( http://evacommentary.org/episode-02/episode-02B-scene6.html#cut166 ), it's either: Aoi Legend (track 26), You are the Only One (track 25) both are sung by Mitsuishi Kotono, or some other piece sung by a different singer (I can't remember the name of either) that was played in episode 23'.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Jul 30, 2008 8:14 pm Ark wrote: By the way the song I'm talking about is the one that Shinji is often playing on his SDAT player. It's also playing in public a lot (e.g. In the store that Asuka runs to after failing in musical twister).

[]:             Quote Post by Ark » Thu Jul 31, 2008 4:43 pm Reichu wrote: Ark wrote: By the way the song I'm talking about is the one that Shinji is often playing on his SDAT player. It's also playing in public a lot (e.g. In the store that Asuka runs to after failing in musical twister).

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Jul 31, 2008 6:18 pm It was released on the 94(? or was it 92) album Lilia From Ys. Evangelion-wise, that was released on this CD http://www.cdjapan.co.jp/detailview.html?KEY=KICA-886 Not to be confused with this release: http://www.cdjapan.co.jp/detailview.html?KEY=KICA-887

[]: Re: Pop song and AC's audio commentary             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri Aug 01, 2008 10:11 am Ark wrote: 2) Does anyone have those audio commentaries for eps 1-6 that Aaron Clark had posted on Evamonkey? It was those that made me want to revisit the series after watching the film years ago.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Fri Aug 01, 2008 10:58 am actually I listened to all 6 about two weeks ago and found them to be mature, measured, entertaining, and informative. I understand that they were a "beta test", of course.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri Aug 01, 2008 11:28 am V wrote: mature...informative

[]:             Quote Post by Ark » Fri Aug 01, 2008 12:56 pm Most people feel embarrased listening to themselves on record. Those 6 are better than most dvd commentaries.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri Aug 01, 2008 3:10 pm Ark wrote: Those 6 are better than most dvd commentaries.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Sat Aug 02, 2008 1:16 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: V wrote: mature...informative
